title: Memory health cards partial and route
analysis:
This task creates a Jinja2 partial template (templates/partials/memory.html) with four health status cards and a GET /partials/memory FastAPI route wired to the existing data module from task 17.

Current state: app.py has lifespan managing httpx.AsyncClient on app.state, Jinja2Templates configured, and only a /api/health route. DashboardConfig exists in config.py but is NOT on app.state yet — must be added as a prerequisite. templates/partials/ directory does not exist. data/memory.py has get_memory_status() and get_queue_stats() fully implemented and tested.

Data shapes (from memory.py and test_memory.py):
- get_memory_status: returns e.g. {graphiti: {connected: bool, node_count: int}, mem0: {connected: bool, memory_count: int}, ...} or {offline: True, error: str}
- get_queue_stats: returns e.g. {counts: {pending: N, retry: N, dead: N, ...}, oldest_pending_age_seconds: float|None} or {offline: True, error: str}
- NOTE: counts dict keys may be sparse (only statuses present in DB). Template must use |default(0) filters.

Starlette 0.52+ uses new TemplateResponse API: templates.TemplateResponse(request, name, context={}) — request is first positional arg, not inside the context dict.

Taskmaster connectivity: The MCP server IS the taskmaster bridge. If the server responds (status not offline), taskmaster is connected. No explicit taskmaster key in get_status response.

Test strategy: mock data functions with unittest.mock.patch at the import boundary in app.py. Data layer already well-tested in test_memory.py. Route tests focus on HTTP response + HTML content assertions.

prerequisites.description: Add app.state.config = DashboardConfig.from_env() to the lifespan in app.py, importing DashboardConfig from dashboard.config. Required before the /partials/memory route can pass config to data functions. Also create the templates/partials/ directory. Existing tests (health endpoint, static files, client fixture) must still pass.
tests: Existing test_scaffold.py tests must still pass

steps.description: Write dashboard/tests/test_memory_partial.py with TestMemoryPartialOnline class. Define module-level mock data: _ONLINE_STATUS = {'graphiti': {'connected': True, 'node_count': 42}, 'mem0': {'connected': True, 'memory_count': 128}} and _ONLINE_QUEUE = {'counts': {'pending': 0, 'retry': 0, 'dead': 0}, 'oldest_pending_age_seconds': None}. Test test_route_returns_200_html: use unittest.mock.patch on 'dashboard.data.memory.get_memory_status' (AsyncMock returning _ONLINE_STATUS) and 'dashboard.data.memory.get_queue_stats' (AsyncMock returning _ONLINE_QUEUE). Use client fixture to GET /partials/memory. Assert status_code == 200 and 'text/html' in content-type header.

Create route and minimal template. In app.py: add imports for Request (fastapi), DashboardConfig (dashboard.config), get_memory_status and get_queue_stats (dashboard.data.memory). Add app.state.config = DashboardConfig.from_env() to lifespan. Add async GET /partials/memory route that gets http_client and config from request.app.state, awaits both data functions, returns templates.TemplateResponse(request, 'partials/memory.html', context={'status': status, 'queue': queue}). Create templates/partials/memory.html with minimal placeholder content (
memory
).

Add tests for online card content in TestMemoryPartialOnline. test_online_contains_card_labels: assert HTML contains 'Graphiti', 'Mem0', 'Taskmaster', 'Write Queue'. test_online_graphiti_count: assert '42' and 'knowledge graph nodes' in HTML. test_online_mem0_count: assert '128' and 'vector memories' in HTML. test_online_taskmaster_connected: assert 'Connected' in HTML.

Build full four-card online template in memory.html. Flex container div (class='flex gap-4'). Each card: div with bg-gray-800 rounded-lg p-6 shadow. Card 1 (Graphiti): status dot (w-3 h-3 rounded-full bg-green-500), 'Graphiti' label (text-sm text-gray-400), node_count as text-3xl font-bold text-white, subtitle 'knowledge graph nodes' (text-xs text-gray-500). Card 2 (Mem0): same pattern with memory_count and 'vector memories'. Card 3 (Taskmaster): dot + label + 'Connected'/'Disconnected' text. Card 4 (Write Queue): dot + label + placeholder metrics line. Use Jinja2 {{ status.graphiti.node_count|default(0) }} pattern.

Add TestMemoryPartialOffline class. test_offline_shows_error_card: mock get_memory_status returning {'offline': True, 'error': 'Connection refused'} and get_queue_stats returning {'offline': True, 'error': 'Connection refused'}. Assert 200 status, HTML contains 'Fused Memory Server Offline' and 'Connection refused'. Assert 'knowledge graph nodes' NOT in HTML (four cards hidden when offline).

Add offline conditional to memory.html. Wrap entire content in: {% if status.offline is defined and status.offline %}...offline card...{% else %}...four cards...{% endif %}. Offline card: single full-width div with bg-red-900/50 border border-red-700 rounded-lg p-6. Heading 'Fused Memory Server Offline' (text-red-200 font-bold text-lg). Error message {{ status.error }} (text-red-300 text-sm mt-2).

Add TestMemoryPartialDots class for status dot colors. test_graphiti_green_when_connected: mock graphiti connected=True, assert 'bg-green-500' in HTML. test_graphiti_red_when_disconnected: mock graphiti connected=False, assert 'bg-red-500' in HTML. test_mem0_red_when_disconnected: mock mem0 connected=False, assert 'bg-red-500' in HTML. test_taskmaster_green_when_online: mock online status (no offline key), assert at least 3 occurrences of 'bg-green-500'.

Implement conditional dot colors in template. Graphiti dot: {% if status.graphiti.connected|default(false) %}bg-green-500{% else %}bg-red-500{% endif %}. Mem0 dot: same pattern with status.mem0.connected. Taskmaster dot: green when server is online (not offline). All dots must be div with w-3 h-3 rounded-full classes.

Add TestWriteQueueCard class. test_queue_dot_green_all_zero: mock counts {pending: 0, retry: 0, dead: 0}, assert 'bg-green-500' for queue card dot. test_queue_dot_yellow_pending: mock counts {pending: 3, retry: 0, dead: 0}, assert 'bg-yellow-500'. test_queue_dot_red_dead: mock counts {pending: 0, retry: 0, dead: 2}, assert 'bg-red-500'. test_queue_metrics_inline: mock counts {pending: 3, retry: 1, dead: 0}, assert '3 pending' and '1 retry' and '0 dead' in HTML.

Implement Write Queue card tri-state dot and inline metrics. Dot: {% if queue.counts.dead|default(0) > 0 %}bg-red-500{% elif queue.counts.pending|default(0) > 0 %}bg-yellow-500{% else %}bg-green-500{% endif %}. Metrics line: '{{ queue.counts.pending|default(0) }} pending · {{ queue.counts.retry|default(0) }} retry · {{ queue.counts.dead|default(0) }} dead'.

Add TestWriteQueueConditionalStyling class. test_dead_count_red_text: mock dead=2, assert 'text-red-' class appears near dead count. test_dead_zero_no_red: mock dead=0, assert dead count is NOT wrapped in red text span. test_oldest_age_shown_over_60: mock oldest_pending_age_seconds=120, assert 'oldest:' and '2m ago' in HTML with 'text-yellow-' styling. test_oldest_age_hidden_under_60: mock oldest_pending_age_seconds=30, assert 'oldest:' NOT in HTML. test_oldest_age_hidden_none: mock oldest_pending_age_seconds=None, assert 'oldest:' NOT in HTML.

Add conditional text styling to Write Queue card. Dead count: {% if queue.counts.dead|default(0) > 0 %}{{ queue.counts.dead }} dead{% else %}0 dead{% endif %}. Oldest age: {% if queue.oldest_pending_age_seconds is not none and queue.oldest_pending_age_seconds > 60 %}
oldest: {{ (queue.oldest_pending_age_seconds // 60)|int }}m ago
{% endif %}.

design_decisions.decision: Add DashboardConfig to app.state in lifespan via from_env()
design_decisions.rationale: The route needs config for data function calls. Storing on app.state alongside http_client is consistent with the existing pattern. from_env() allows env var overrides in production.

design_decisions.decision: Use Starlette 0.52+ TemplateResponse API: templates.TemplateResponse(request, name, context={})
design_decisions.rationale: Starlette 0.52 deprecated old-style TemplateResponse(name, {'request': request}). New API has request as first positional arg, context as keyword. Avoids DeprecationWarnings.

design_decisions.decision: Mock data functions at dashboard.data.memory module level, not at app module level
design_decisions.rationale: Patching at the source module (dashboard.data.memory.get_memory_status) is more robust since it doesn't depend on import style in app.py. unittest.mock.patch resolves the name at the source.

design_decisions.decision: Template is a standalone HTMX partial, not extending base.html
design_decisions.rationale: This partial returns an HTML fragment for HTMX swapping into a parent page. It should NOT extend base.html or include <html>/<head>/<body> tags — just the card markup.

design_decisions.decision: Check status.offline for the offline/online branching
design_decisions.rationale: Both data functions connect to the same fused-memory server. If get_memory_status fails, get_queue_stats almost certainly will too. Checking status.offline is sufficient to gate between the offline error card and the four online cards.

design_decisions.decision: Derive Taskmaster connected state from server reachability
design_decisions.rationale: get_status response has no explicit 'taskmaster' key. The MCP server IS the taskmaster bridge. If the server responds with data (not offline), taskmaster is connected.

design_decisions.decision: Use Jinja2 |default(0) for queue count keys
design_decisions.rationale: get_queue_stats builds counts from DB rows grouped by status — only statuses with >0 count may appear as keys. Template must use |default(0) for safe access to pending, retry, dead.

design_decisions.decision: Separate test file test_memory_partial.py for route+template tests
design_decisions.rationale: test_memory.py tests data module (MCP transport + data functions). test_memory_partial.py tests route+template integration. Different concerns warrant separate files.

design_decisions.decision: Use integer floor division for oldest age display
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec says 'oldest: Xm ago' implying minutes. Floor division (oldest_pending_age_seconds // 60) gives a clean integer minute count appropriate for a dashboard glance metric.

reuse.how: Import and call in the route handler. These handle all errors internally, returning {offline: True, error: str} on failure — no error handling needed in the route.

reuse.what: Jinja2Templates instance (templates variable) already in app.py
reuse.how: Use templates.TemplateResponse(request, 'partials/memory.html', context={...}) to render the partial.
Route accesses via request.app.state.http_client and passes it to data functions. Already managed by lifespan.
Import in app.py, call from_env() in lifespan to populate app.state.config.
Use the 'client' fixture in route tests. It creates TestClient wrapping the app with proper lifespan, so app.state.http_client and config are available.

reuse.what: Test data shape patterns from test_memory.py
reuse.how: Reference for realistic mock return values. Adapt for route test mock data constants.

reuse.what: Dark theme Tailwind classes from base.html
reuse.how: Follow same palette: bg-gray-800 for cards, text-gray-100/400/500 for text hierarchy, border-gray-700 for borders.
